# Beudox HR Portal - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a single-page application (SPA) built with React and TypeScript, using Supabase as the backend-as-a-service platform. The application follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns.

### Core Architecture Patterns

- **Component Composition**: Extensive use of composition over inheritance, with reusable UI components
- **Custom Hooks**: Business logic abstracted into custom hooks for reusability
- **Context Providers**: React Context used for global state management (auth, employee data)
- **Route-based Pages**: Next.js-style pages router with React Router for navigation
- **Database-first Design**: Schema-driven development with Supabase PostgreSQL

### Key Files and Their Purposes

#### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap, renders root App component
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Main dashboard/home page component
- `src/App.tsx`: Root application component with routing setup

#### Core Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main layout wrapper with sidebar and top navigation
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar with role-based menu items
- `src/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`: Top navigation bar with notifications and user menu
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Brand logo component with variant support
- `src/components/MandatoryPasswordChange.tsx`: Secure password change modal for new users

#### Business Logic Components
- `src/components/attendance/AttendanceSummary.tsx`: Complex analytics component for attendance data
- `src/components/employee-profile/`: Suite of components for employee profile management
- `src/components/leave/`: Leave request and balance management components
- `src/components/payroll/`: Payroll processing and history components
- `src/components/settings/`: Administrative settings panels

#### Utility Libraries
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Common utility functions (cn for className merging)
- `src/lib/attendance-format.ts`: Attendance data formatting functions
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Role-based access control utilities
- `src/lib/notifications.ts`: Notification sending logic
- `src/integrations/supabase/`: Supabase client configuration and type definitions

## Database Schema and Relationships

### Core Entities

- **employees**: User profiles with authentication links
- **attendance_records**: Daily attendance data with timestamps
- **leave_requests**: Leave applications with approval workflow
- **payroll_records**: Salary and overtime calculations
- **projects**: Project management with team assignments
- **evaluations**: Performance review cycles
- **company_settings**: Configurable company parameters

### Key Relationships

- Employees belong to companies and have roles
- Attendance records link employees to daily time entries
- Leave requests reference employees and leave types
- Payroll records calculate based on attendance and employee salary data
- Projects have many-to-many relationships with employees
- Evaluations track performance over time with salary implications

## Business Logic and Data Flow

### Authentication Flow
- Users authenticate via Supabase Auth
- New users must change temporary passwords via MandatoryPasswordChange
- JWT tokens manage session state
- Role-based access controls restrict feature visibility

### Attendance Processing
- Records capture check-in/check-out times
- Automatic calculations for working hours, overtime, and lateness
- Holiday and weekend detection affects overtime rates
- Summary analytics identify patterns and anomalies

### Leave Management
- Employees request leave against configured leave types
- Approval workflow with balance tracking
- Automatic balance calculations based on working days

### Payroll Generation
- Monthly payroll based on attendance records
- Overtime calculations (regular and holiday rates)
- Salary history tracking with increment proposals

## Configuration and Environment

### Build Configuration
- Vite for fast development and optimized production builds
- TypeScript for type safety
- Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling
- ESLint for code quality

### Environment Variables
- Supabase project configuration (URL, keys)
- Build-time environment variables prefixed with VITE_

### UI Component System
- shadcn/ui for consistent, accessible components
- Radix UI primitives for complex interactions
- Custom design tokens for brand consistency
- Responsive design with mobile-first approach

## Development Workflow

### State Management
- React hooks for local component state
- Context API for global application state
- Supabase real-time subscriptions for live data updates
- Optimistic updates for better UX

### Error Handling
- Try-catch blocks in async operations
- User-friendly error messages
- Loading states with skeleton components
- Graceful degradation for network failures

### Performance Considerations
- Code splitting with dynamic imports
- Lazy loading of heavy components
- Efficient re-renders with React.memo
- Database query optimization with proper indexing

## Security Considerations

- Row Level Security (RLS) policies in Supabase
- Role-based access control throughout the application
- Secure password policies with strength validation
- Input sanitization and validation
- JWT token management for API access

## Testing Strategy

- Unit tests for utility functions and hooks
- Component tests for UI behavior
- Integration tests for API interactions
- E2E tests for critical user workflows
- Playwright for browser automation testing

This technical context provides the foundation for understanding the codebase structure, business logic, and development patterns used in the Beudox HR Portal application.
